component.name: Hikyaku
component.summary: Matrix chat client with activity awareness

description.p: Hikyaku is a Matrix chat client for GNOME built around activity awareness. It helps you stay on top of conversations by surfacing the rooms and direct messages that matter most, and lets an on-device AI assistant answer questions about Matrix and summarize room conversations — all without your data leaving your device.

ul.li: End-to-end encrypted messaging with key backup and recovery
Direct messages and group rooms with space organisation
On-device AI assistant powered by Ollama (optional)
Message bookmarks and room pinning
Spell-check and rich text rendering
@mention notifications and unread badges

description.p: Follow-up fixes to v0.4.0 for scroll behaviour and message rendering in busy channels.

ul.li: Auto-scroll no longer fights the user reading history. Previously an incoming message could snap the viewport back to the tail even when the user had scrolled up to compose a reply to an older message; the "near bottom" heuristic used a generous 150px slack that treated "reading recent context" as "pinned to tail." Now uses a strict 20px check — only actually-at-the-tail state triggers auto-scroll on new arrivals.

Messages from clients that put reply content only in HTML (some variants of Element X / Cinny — plain-text body is only the fallback quote block) no longer render as zero-length rows. The first-paint fallback now extracts plain text from the formatted_body via a lightweight HTML stripper when the plain body is empty or whitespace-only, so the row always has visible content.

Scroll on busy public channels (GNOME OS scale — many small events per second: typing indicators, reactions, read receipts) is now smoother. The GTK-side matrix-event drainer yields to input processing after every event during active user scroll (was every 4 events); events are never dropped, they just get more scheduling breathing room so the scroll gesture is not disrupted by concurrent tokio activity.

description.p: Historical-scroll oscillation fix and architectural cleanup.

ul.li: Fixed the long-standing "row oscillation and frame stutter when scrolling through historical messages" bug. Root cause: matrix pagination RPC (~350 ms of tokio-side work) overlapping with the user's active scroll gesture caused visible frame stutter, even though tokio and GTK run on separate threads. Fix: debounce the fetch-older RPC until scroll settles (last input quiet for 200 ms, 4.5 s cap).
Cold avatar decode moved off the GTK thread into a dedicated worker. Every historical sender's first appearance no longer costs a synchronous ~1400 µs PNG decode on the render thread.

Event delivery from the matrix thread to the GTK loop is now split into three prioritized channels (critical/bulk/plugin) so login, sync, msg-state, and decryption events cannot queue behind ephemeral typing indicators or plugin traffic under load.

Timeline extracted to its own crate (crates/timeline) with a fuzz-style invariant test harness that runs 4 000 random-op iterations plus pagination and gap-fill scenarios, verifying sort / uniqueness / index consistency / cached derived-state invariants after every mutation.
Same-position pagination gap-fills batched into a single splice per unique insert position; a pagination that used to fire 200 separate items_changed emissions now fires one per unique gap position.
Same-sender bind skip: consecutive messages from one person no longer re-run the avatar / flag / flair widget updates on every widget recycle.
Set-selectable and timestamp label writes cached across rebinds; markup shepherd applies rendered Pango markup directly to the label instead of triggering a full rebind cascade.

description.p: Timeline architecture overhaul plus a swathe of jitter and reactions fixes.

ul.li: New Timeline GObject as the single chokepoint for per-room state: sort-by-timestamp, dedup-by-event-id, and index-in-sync invariants are now enforced at every mutation, eliminating an entire class of "reactions vanish, messages out of order, echoes duplicate" bugs.

Reactions now render live via a reactive notify handler — previously reactions arriving via sync could land on the message but never repaint the visible pill row if the row was off-screen or belonged to a cached non-current room.

Scroll jitter substantially reduced: bulk-splice tail eviction, single-splice room switch, coalesced async-markup drain that defers itself if scroll is still active, and a per-room per-row height estimate for pagination scroll restore.
Pagination is snappier: prefetch fires when scrolled within one viewport of the top instead of exactly at the top; the fetching-older cooldown dropped from 3000 ms to 300 ms.

Historical timeline loads correctly past the local-cache overlap window: the server-side pagination now loops with the running end token until it returns a chunk genuinely older than what the UI already has.
New-message tint applies on dedup-hit so the "unread while unfocused" highlight isn't silently dropped when bg_refresh pre-populates the timeline before the live sync event arrives.
Rust dependencies now build at opt-level 1 in debug (via .cargo/config.toml.example), removing a major contributor to release-build-only smoothness.
Peak memory reduced from ~4 GB to ~70 MB by sharing one EmojiChooser across all message rows instead of one per row, and by evicting cold per-room widget trees via an LRU cache.
Room entry and scroll: HTML→Pango conversion rewritten to byte-based scanning, cutting the worst per-message parse from ~38 ms to under 200 µs.
Smooth scrolling: GSK renderer pinned to Vulkan, removing per-frame offscreen fallback for GskTransformNode / GskMaskNode / GskContainerNode on affected drivers.
Room list: decoded avatar textures are now cached, so scrolling the sidebar no longer re-decodes images per row.
Reaction pills are pooled per row and reused across rebinds instead of being destroyed and recreated.
Added a scope-guard timing helper (src/perf.rs) and comprehensive instrumentation across the bind, room-entry, and focus-activation hot paths.
